title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A fragile two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran took effect on April 7, 2026, after President Trump's deadline for capitulation and Pakistani mediation, with Tehran agreeing to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and Washington halting attacks alongside Israel. This followed weeks of escalating conflict sparked by Israeli strikes on Iran, amid US claims of destroying much of Iran's defense-industrial base. Reports indicate the truce faces immediate strains, including limited tanker transits and ongoing Israeli actions in Lebanon against Hezbollah. Delegations convene in Islamabad on April 10 for talks based on competing 15-point and 10-point proposals, testing prospects for de-escalation in this high-stakes diplomacy.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler's trader consensus at 22% implied probability to claim a third green jacket stems from his unmatched Augusta National record—wins in 2022 and 2024, plus top-10s in recent starts—bolstered by elite strokes gained around-the-green stats despite a three-week layoff after withdrawing from the Houston Open for his second child's birth. Defending 2025 champion Rory McIlroy sits at 10.5% amid solid strokes gained off-the-tee form but lengthened odds from prior favoritism, while Ludvig Aberg garners 8.8% support via runner-up debut in 2024, T7 last year, and three straight top-5s entering dry, firm conditions favoring precise ball-strikers. Xander Schauffele and Tommy Fleetwood trail with strong recent tee-to-green play, highlighting a competitive field where course history and par-5 scoring differentiate leaders.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 15.4% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, buoyed by their dominant UEFA qualifying campaign and Euro 2024 triumph, showcasing midfield mastery from Rodri and Yamal's emergence. France trails closely at 14.6%, reflecting Mbappé-led attacking depth and consistent Nations League form, while England's 11.3% underscores squad talent under new management despite tricky group draw foes. Argentina (9.0%) and Brazil (8.6%) remain viable via CONMEBOL supremacy, but aging stars like Messi temper enthusiasm. Recent European playoffs—Sweden's dramatic 3-2 extra-time win over Poland, Türkiye and Czechia's advances—highlight elite parity in the expanded 48-team field, fostering a bunched race with no dominant favorite amid balanced group stage paths.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices California Governor Gavin Newsom as the narrow 2028 Democratic presidential nominee frontrunner at 26.5%, reflecting his executive experience, national visibility as a Trump administration critic, and leads in recent March 2026 polls among California Democrats over Kamala Harris. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.7% on strong progressive grassroots appeal but polarizing national ratings, while Georgia Senator Jon Ossoff's 6% stems from battleground state credibility and moderate positioning. The wide-open field post-Harris's 2024 defeat lacks a clear incumbent path; 2026 midterm outcomes for red-state Democrats like Ossoff or governors such as Shapiro, Whitmer, or Newsom could consolidate support via fundraising surges, early primary polling, and party endorsements before Iowa caucuses.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. holds trader consensus at 49% implied probability for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination on Polymarket, narrowly ahead of Vice President J.D. Vance at 39.4% and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 21.1%, reflecting early speculation on Trump's potential successors amid his term limits. Recent HHS management enhancements and RFK Jr.'s midterm travel push to promote the Make America Healthy Again agenda have bolstered his outsider appeal, despite growing Republican resistance and eroding public trust in health agencies. Meanwhile, Vance and Rubio's differing postures on the Iran conflict—highlighted in early April reporting—and Trump's informal polling of donors favoring Rubio over Vance keep the field competitive. This tight race hinges on Trump's endorsement signals, midterm outcomes, and cabinet performances, with primaries still over two years away.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 18.8% implied probability for the 2028 presidential winner, buoyed by recent GOP primary polls like March surveys naming him Republicans' top pick and his role enforcing the Trump agenda amid 2026 midterm preparations. California Governor Gavin Newsom trails closely at 16.4%, reflecting his frontrunner status in early Democratic rankings and public openness to a White House bid post-2026 elections. The race remains tight due to the long timeline to primaries, fluid party fields after Trump's term limits, and uncertainties from upcoming midterms that could test turnout in swing states and reveal nominee frontrunners; separation may emerge from strong midterm performances, Trump endorsements, policy wins like foreign policy de-escalations, or scandals shifting polling averages.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 9?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: A two-week US-Iran ceasefire, agreed April 7 via Pakistani mediation, aims to pause direct hostilities from the war sparked by US-Israeli strikes on February 28, with talks slated to start in Islamabad on April 11 over Strait of Hormuz access and proxy restraint. Yet strains emerged within 48 hours as Israel conducted over 100 airstrikes in Lebanon targeting Hezbollah, killing more than 250 and prompting Iranian accusations of violation—claims dismissed by US and Israeli officials who exclude Lebanon from the deal. This reflects trader consensus on prolonged uncertainty, as no continuous 14-day lull in qualifying military actions has materialized amid ongoing regional escalations and diplomatic hurdles.

title: Indian Premier League: Kolkata Knight Riders vs Lucknow Super Giants
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Kolkata Knight Riders and Lucknow Super Giants scheduled for April 9 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Kolkata Knight Riders vs Lucknow Super Giants - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Kolkata Knight Riders and Lucknow Super Giants scheduled for 2026-04-09 in Indian Premier League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Kolkata Knight Riders vs Lucknow Super Giants - Completed match?
markets.description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Kolkata Knight Riders and Lucknow Super Giants scheduled for 2026-04-09 in Indian Premier League is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kolkata Knight Riders enter their IPL 2026 home clash against Lucknow Super Giants at Eden Gardens winless after losses to MI and SRH plus a no-result versus PBKS, severely depleted by pace injuries to Harshit Rana and Akash Deep, with Matheesha Pathirana only partially available. Sunil Narine's return bolsters spin options, while Cameron Green eyes a bowling comeback post-recovery, but top-order woes persist under captain Ajinkya Rahane. LSG, buoyed by a recent win under Rishabh Pant, hold momentum with Nicholas Pooran key in the middle order and a potent attack featuring Mohammed Shami and Avesh Khan. An orange weather alert raises rain and washout risks on a typically high-scoring pitch, where LSG leads head-to-head 4-2.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 98% implied probability of no change in the federal funds rate at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, backed by the Federal Reserve's steady 3.5%-3.75% target range from its March 17-18 decision and resilient economic data. March nonfarm payrolls surged 178,000—exceeding forecasts—with unemployment dipping to 4.3%, while annual CPI accelerated to 3.3% amid oil price spikes from geopolitical tensions, reinforcing the pause amid sticky inflation and a robust labor market. This skin-in-the-game positioning echoes CME FedWatch odds near 98%, with the dot plot signaling just one 2026 cut later. Realistic challenges include softer April jobs or CPI prints ahead of the meeting, potentially prompting a dovish reassessment, though current momentum favors continuity.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Hungary's parliamentary election set for April 12, trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Péter Magyar at 69.5% implied probability to become the next prime minister, reflecting recent polling averages where his center-right Tisza party leads Viktor Orbán's Fidesz by 49% to 41%, including a March Medián survey showing Tisza up 58%-35% among decided voters. Magyar, a former Fidesz insider turned opposition leader, has unified anti-Orbán forces amid voter fatigue after 16 years of Fidesz rule, bolstered by pro-EU messaging and large rallies. Orbán trails at 30.5% despite the incumbent-friendly electoral system featuring single-member districts and compensatory lists, with final campaigning intensified by U.S. Vice President JD Vance's endorsement visit. Post-election coalition negotiations could influence government formation, keeping the outcome competitive.

title: Counter-Strike: TheMongolz vs PARIVISION (BO3) - PGL Bucharest Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Quarterfinal 4 match between TheMongolz and PARIVISION in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "TheMongolz" if TheMongolz win the match against PARIVISION.

This market will resolve to "PARIVISION" if PARIVISION win the match against TheMongolz.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map Handicap: PRV (-1.5) vs TheMongolz (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Quarterfinal 4 match between TheMongolz and PARIVISION in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "PARIVISION" if PARIVISION wins 2 or more maps than TheMongolz in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "TheMongolz". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: TheMongolz vs PARIVISION (BO3) - PGL Bucharest Playoffs
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Quarterfinal 4 match between TheMongolz and PARIVISION in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "TheMongolz" if TheMongolz win the match against PARIVISION.

This market will resolve to "PARIVISION" if PARIVISION win the match against TheMongolz.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Quarterfinal 4 match between TheMongolz and PARIVISION in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if TheMongolz and PARIVISION play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

eventMetadata.context_description: In the PGL Bucharest 2026 playoffs quarterfinal BO3, pre-tournament favorites TheMongolz (world #8/#9 VRS) and surging PARIVISION (#3/#7 VRS) clash after resilient group stage paths. TheMongolz advanced 3-1, highlighted by 2-0 wins over BC.Game, EYEBALLERS, and Wildcard despite an 0-2 loss to Astralis, showcasing strong momentum. PARIVISION went 3-2, slogging past Wildcard 2-0 on April 8 for the final spot following losses to 3DMAX and FUT but upsets over Legacy and FOKUS. No prior head-to-head; map veto (PARIVISION Dust2 pick, TheMongolz Mirage, Ancient decider) favors PARIVISION's superior win rates (65% Dust2, 82% Ancient past 3 months). Traders focus on roster stability—no changes noted—and current CS2 meta emphasizing eco execution and T-side aggression amid single-elimination pressure.

title: UEFA Champions League Winner
description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

markets.question: Will Real Madrid win the 2025–26 Champions League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich's 2-1 away victory over Real Madrid and Arsenal's dramatic 1-0 stoppage-time win at Sporting CP in UEFA Champions League quarter-final first legs on April 7 have propelled them to the top of trader consensus at 29.5% and 28.5% implied probabilities, reflecting their advantageous positions ahead of home second legs. PSG's commanding 2-0 home win against Liverpool bolsters their 20.5% standing, while Atletico Madrid's shock 2-0 triumph at Barcelona elevates them to 7.4%, underscoring the knockout phase's volatility. With aggregate leads slim and second legs on April 14-15 looming, the market remains tightly contested, as comebacks, home advantage, and form could reshape paths to the semi-finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder top trader consensus at 40.5% implied probability for the 2026 NBA championship, fueled by their league-best 64-16 record, clinched No. 1 Western Conference seed, and seven-game win streak including blowouts over the Clippers (128-110 on April 8) and Lakers (123-87 on April 7), showcasing Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play amid a healthy young core primed for playoff home-court dominance. San Antonio Spurs follow at 15.3% after securing 61-19 for No. 2 West, bolstered by Victor Wembanyama's Defensive Player of the Year award and recent 112-101 win over Portland, though an overtime loss to Denver highlights vulnerabilities. Boston Celtics' 11.8% reflects their 54-25 mark and four straight wins like 113-102 versus Charlotte, positioning them as East contenders behind Detroit's league-high East wins, while Nuggets' nine-game streak supports 10.5% amid the tight playoff picture with first round nearing April 18.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 3 - April 10, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 3 12:00 PM ET to April 10, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Elon Musk posting 240-259 tweets from April 3 to April 10, 2026, at 60.5% implied probability, driven by his steady pace of 30-42 daily posts so far—totaling 216 through early April 9, averaging 31 per day across six full periods. This reflects consistent social media engagement amid ongoing xAI developments and Tesla updates, without major spikes from viral controversies or events in the past week. The 260-279 range at 21.5% captures upside risk if Musk ramps up replies or announcements, while lower buckets like 220-239 (12%) hedge quieter finishes. With under 24 hours left until market close on April 10, any late surge in posts could shift odds, but historical weekly patterns around 200-300 reinforce the frontrunner's positioning.

title: Counter-Strike: FUT Esports vs B8 (BO3) - PGL Bucharest Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Quarterfinal 3 match between FUT Esports and B8 in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "FUT Esports" if FUT Esports win the match against B8.

This market will resolve to "B8" if B8 win the match against FUT Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: FUT Esports, ranked #13 on HLTV, enters this PGL Bucharest 2026 playoff quarterfinal BO3 as the higher-seeded squad with a 7-3 record over their last 10 matches, including a 2-1 group stage win over 3DMAX and a dominant 2-0 over NRG. B8, at #18, rides momentum from securing playoffs via a 2-0 upset of Legacy yesterday, plus 2-0 victories against Inner Circle and Wildcard in Swiss rounds, showcasing esenthial's standout form. Head-to-head splits 1-1 recently, with both rosters stable—no transfers or benchings noted. Map pool favors FUT's Anubis/Dust2 edges, but B8's underdog aggression could force close series in single-elimination playoffs.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil spot prices have surged with extreme volatility in early April 2026, peaking above $117 per barrel on April 7 amid heightened Middle East geopolitical tensions before plunging nearly 16% to around $91 on April 8 following de-escalation agreements that eased risk premiums, stabilizing near $97/bbl as of April 9. Bearish supply dynamics dominate trader sentiment, including a 3.1 million barrel U.S. crude inventory build to 464.7 million barrels for the week ended April 3 per latest EIA data—exceeding expectations—and OPEC+ production quota hikes by over 200,000 barrels per day extending into May. Upcoming EIA inventory reports on April 15 and any flare-ups in regional conflicts could sway the April price range, with the futures curve pricing June 2026 delivery at roughly $99/bbl amid softening global demand signals.

title: Next Prime Minister of Denmark?
description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed as Prime Minister of Denmark following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as Prime Minister by the Danish monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed as Prime Minister of Denmark following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as Prime Minister by the Danish monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats secured the largest bloc with 50 seats in the March 24, 2026, Folketing election despite vote share losses, positioning her as the dominant force in ongoing coalition negotiations now entering their third week. As caretaker leader following the traditional post-election government resignation to the king, Frederiksen benefits from her party's lead, historical dealmaking prowess in Denmark's proportional representation system, and disarray among rivals like the Liberals (23 seats) and Moderates (16 seats led by Lars Løkke Rasmussen). Trader consensus at 93.5% reflects expectations of her securing a minority government or centrist-left coalition, bolstered by external pressures like U.S. rhetoric on Greenland unifying support. Realistic challenges include prolonged talks collapsing into a centrist alternative excluding Social Democrats or a no-confidence trigger, though historical precedents favor the largest party prevailing.

title: LoL: Weibo Gaming vs Ninjas in Pyjamas (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 9 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Weibo Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 32.5 in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 9 at 5:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 33 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: LoL: Weibo Gaming vs Ninjas in Pyjamas (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 9 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Weibo Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?

eventMetadata.context_description: Weibo Gaming enters as the pedigree favorite in LPL Group Ascend BO3 action, leveraging their veteran roster of top laner Zika, jungler Jiejie, mid laner Xiaohu, ADC Elk, and support Hang, plus a strong 3rd-place Split 1 playoffs run including a 3-2 upset over Top Esports. However, WBG's sluggish Split 2 start—0-2 series record after losses to Anyone's Legend and prior foes—has cooled trader consensus amid shaky macro execution. Ninjas in Pyjamas counters with revamped lineup featuring Korean imports top laner HOYA and jungler Guwon (recent POM), alongside bot duo Assum-Zhuo shining in a 2-0 rout of Team WE and competitive 2-1 versus Invictus Gaming last 48 hours. BO3 volatility, head-to-head history favoring WBG (56% win rate), and early standings pressure (WBG 0-1 pre-match, NIP 1-1) heighten upset potential.

title: Colombia Presidential Election
description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M a narrow edge at 49.5% to win Colombia's presidential election, with Iván Cepeda Castro at 42.5% and Paloma Valencia at 41.6%, reflecting a fragmented race likely headed to a June 21 runoff after the May 31 first round. Recent AtlasIntel polling confirms an open field, with top contenders under 30% amid polarization between Cepeda's left-wing Historic Pact—bolstered by their March 8 congressional primary win and assembly majority—and Valencia's center-right Democratic Centre surge post her primary victory, while Abelardo de la Espriella fades to 14.5%. The tight dynamics stem from undecided voters (10-20% in March surveys), Petro administration scandals eroding left support, and voter priorities on violence, corruption, and economy. Separation could arise from televised debates, regional endorsements, turnout in battlegrounds like Antioquia, or late security incidents.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's Linda Lampenius & Pete Parkkonen top Polymarket's Eurovision 2026 winner odds at 39% implied probability following their commanding UMK victory in late February with the high-energy "Liekinheitin," a Finnish-language rock ballad blending powerful vocals and violin that has fueled broad jury and televote appeal in early previews. Trader consensus reflects the duo's polished staging and cultural momentum, positioning them as clear frontrunners amid ongoing national selections. Denmark (10.5%) and France (10.2%) trail closely with upbeat pop entries from their respective finals, while Australia's Delta Goodrem and Greece's dance hit gain traction via streaming buzz. With Vienna semifinals four weeks away on May 12, rehearsal footage and final running order could spark shifts in this crowded field.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. airstrikes targeted over 50 military sites on Kharg Island, Iran's critical oil export terminal in the Persian Gulf, on April 7, 2026, amid escalating tensions over the Strait of Hormuz closure, but spared oil and gas infrastructure to avoid broader economic disruption. Iranian state media asserts the island remains fully under armed forces control, with exports continuing uninterrupted, following earlier March raids that caused no lasting impact. President Trump's deadline for a nuclear or regional deal looms, potentially prompting further military action or diplomacy, while traders weigh invasion risks against historical reluctance for ground operations in such chokepoints. No confirmed loss of control has occurred, keeping resolution distant.

title: Who will be confirmed as Fed Chair?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next individual formally confirmed as Chair of the Federal Reserve.

Formal confirmation as Chair of the Federal Reserve requires the Senate to confirm a nominee as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count. Senate confirmation of a listed individual as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

If no Senate confirmation for the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve has occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's formal nomination of former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh as the next Federal Reserve Chair on March 4 has propelled trader consensus to imply a 96% probability of confirmation, reflecting his extensive central banking experience from 2006–2011 and alignment with administration priorities on monetary policy. The process advances with a Senate Banking Committee hearing scheduled for April 16, ahead of Jerome Powell's term expiration on May 15, amid White House confidence in a timely Senate vote under Republican control. While GOP support appears solid, potential challenges include holds from senators like Thom Tillis over ongoing investigations into Powell, unexpected scandals, or procedural delays that could extend the vacancy or prompt an acting chair appointment.

title: Rolex Monte Carlo Masters: Carlos Alcaraz vs Tomas Etcheverry
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Carlos Alcaraz and Tomas Etcheverry in the Rolex Monte Carlo Masters, scheduled for April 9 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Carlos Alcaraz' if Carlos Alcaraz advances against Tomas Etcheverry.

This market will resolve to 'Tomas Etcheverry' if Tomas Etcheverry advances against Carlos Alcaraz.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Carlos Alcaraz vs. Tomas Etcheverry: Total Sets O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Carlos Alcaraz and Tomas Etcheverry in the Rolex Monte Carlo Masters, scheduled for April 9 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of sets completed equals or exceeds 3. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
A super tie breaker is considered as one (1) set for total set markets.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Rolex Monte Carlo Masters: Carlos Alcaraz vs Tomas Etcheverry Total Sets: O/U 2.5

markets.question: Alcaraz vs. Etcheverry: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Carlos Alcaraz and Tomas Etcheverry in the Rolex Monte Carlo Masters, scheduled for April 9 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending champion and world No. 1 Carlos Alcaraz holds a commanding edge over Tomas Martin Etcheverry in their ATP Masters 1000 Monte-Carlo Masters round-of-16 clash on clay, buoyed by his 84.4% career clay win rate and a 1-0 head-to-head lead. Alcaraz advanced with a dominant 6-1, 6-3 second-round win over Sebastian Baez, extending his clay streak to 14 matches amid strong early-season form after injury recoveries. Etcheverry, a clay specialist with a 10-2 record on the surface in 2026, upset Terence Atmane in the prior round but faces a stylistic mismatch against Alcaraz's explosive baseline game and superior rankings. No injury concerns reported, with quarterfinal potential against Bublik or Lehecka adding stakes ahead of Roland Garros prep.

title: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by ...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Trump, the US government, or the military publicly and officially announce that their military operations against Iran, initiated on February 28, 2026, have concluded by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying statements must clearly indicate that the operation has ended. Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources or leaks will not qualify. 

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g. posts from his personal Truth Social account), will count. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump announced a conditional two-week ceasefire with Iran on April 7, 2026, averting escalation ahead of his self-imposed deadline on strikes against Iranian infrastructure, including the Strait of Hormuz. This follows U.S. and Israeli airstrikes since February 28 that Trump claims have achieved core objectives, severely degrading Iran's navy, air force, missile capabilities, and leadership after over 13,000 targets hit. Negotiations begin Friday in Islamabad, potentially paving the way for de-escalation, though the truce is fragile amid Israeli actions in Lebanon and mutual claims of victory. No formal announcement has confirmed the end of U.S. military operations, with traders awaiting an explicit public statement from Trump, the White House, or Pentagon resolving the market.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Keiko Fujimori leads Polymarket trader consensus at 35.5% implied probability to win Peru's presidency, buoyed by her steady first-round polling lead of 16-18% in early April Ipsos, CPI, and Datum surveys amid a fragmented 35-candidate field poised for a June 7 runoff. Former Lima mayor Ricardo Belmont has surged to 10-13% on anti-corruption appeals, positioning him second at 18.3%, while comedian Carlos Álvarez (17.7%) and far-right Rafael López Aliaga (16.5%) vie closely behind with outsider momentum despite López Aliaga's recent slippage. Surging crime and corruption concerns dominate voter priorities after a decade of instability with nine presidents since 2016; final debates emphasized security reforms, but high undecideds (20%+) keep the race volatile ahead of the April 12 first round.

title: Rayo Vallecano de Madrid vs. AEK
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Europa Conference League game, scheduled for Thursday, April 9, 2026 between Rayo Vallecano de Madrid and AEK.

markets.question: Will Rayo Vallecano de Madrid vs. AEK end in a draw?

In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 9, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".

eventMetadata.context_description: **Rayo Vallecano's dominant 3-0 lead in the 75th minute against AEK Athens in the UEFA Conference League quarter-final first leg at Estadio de Vallecas has driven trader consensus to imply a 99.7% probability of a home win.** Isi Palazón's penalty sealed the third goal amid total Rayo control, with Vallecas erupting in support and AEK struggling for shots on target despite topping the Greek Super League table. Rayo, mid-table in La Liga, leverages home advantage and recent European momentum, while AEK faces travel fatigue. A realistic upset requires AEK scoring four unanswered goals in 15 minutes plus stoppage—possible via red cards or defensive collapse, but highly improbable given Rayo's defensive solidity and the wisdom of crowds pricing minimal risk.

eventMetadata.context_description: Philadelphia 76ers face the Houston Rockets at Toyota Center on April 9 in a late-season clash with playoff implications, as the 43-36 Sixers fight to secure a top-6 Eastern Conference spot and avoid the play-in tournament. Joel Embiid's late ruling out due to illness—announced hours before tipoff—joins Cam Payne (hamstring) and Johni Broome (knee) on the sidelines, severely depleting Philadelphia's frontcourt depth behind Paul George and Tyrese Maxey. Meanwhile, the 50-29 Rockets, surging on a seven-game win streak and chasing Western Conference home-court advantage, welcome rested stars like Kevin Durant and Alperen Sengun despite absences of Fred VanVleet (knee) and Steven Adams (ankle), with Tari Eason questionable (illness). Houston's home dominance and momentum shape trader consensus amid the Sixers' must-win pressure.

title: Bitcoin price on April 9?
description:
This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.
